ASHLAND FOREST SERVICE REPORTED APPROX 200 TREES DOWN ON DUPREY RD EAGLE LAKE. OFF RTE 11 ACROSS FROM FISH RVR HEALTH CENTER. TRACTOR TRAILER BLOWN OVER. REPORTED STONES BLOWN OVER IN CATHOLIC CEMETERY.

TORNADO DAMAGE IN THE CHUTE AREA OF EASTON. SEVERAL WITNESSES REPORT MERGING FUNNEL CLOUDS REACHING THE GROUND AND DEBRIS BEING LIFTED INTO THE SKY. NUMEROUS TREES KNOCKED DOWN, WITH A FEW SNAPPED OFF AT 10-15 FEET. METAL ROOF PEALED OFF DAIRY BARN AND BARN DOORS BLOWN OFF ANOTHER OUTBUILDING.
MANY TREES DOWN ON 1A JUST SOUTH OF EASTON CENTER

NWS STORM SURVEY,RADAR DATA, AND SPOTTER REPORTS INDICATE A FUNNEL CLOUD BRIEFLY REACHED TREE TOP LEVELS RESULTING IN SEVERAL SNAPPED TREE TOPS. THIS EVENT WILL BE RATED AND AN EF0 TORNADO WITH A SPORATIC 0.6 MILE PATH THAT WAS UP TO 50 YARDS WIDE. THE EVENT OCCURED IN THE VICINITY OF SCHOOL STREET JUST SOUTH-EAST OF THE TOWN OF STOCKHOLM.

ST. JUSTE POE RECORDED 3.77 INCHES OF RAIN IN LESS THAN 6 HOURS. RUNOFF FROM THIS HEAVY RAIN UNDERMINED THE SAINT JUSTE ROAD CAUSING A SINKHOLE THAT CLOSED THE INTERNATIONAL BORDER FOR MOST OF THE WEEKEND.
SAINT AURELIE ROAD FLOODED AND DAMAGED BY FLASH FLOODING. RAINFALL ESTIMATED 2.50 INCHES IN LESS THAN 6 HOURS.
